rulings.Case Title: MOLALLA HOLDINGS, INC. DBA vs. KARO, STEVEN IND/DBA PROPERTY MAINTENANCE SERVICE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Renewal Of Default, Original Entry Of Default And Original Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 16, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT KARO, STEVEN IND/DBA PROPERTY MAINTENANCE SERVICE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Renewal Of Default, Original Entry Of Default And Original Default Granted. Plaintiff's service by publication, even though permitted by the court on an unopposed application, requires that the default and default judgment be set aside because at the time that service by publication was made, plaintiff knew defendant's residence address yet neither served defendant by mail as required by section 415.30 or, once service by publication was accomplished, served a copyu of the publication order by mail as required by section 415.50(b) and the order providing for service by publication (Code of Civil Procedure 473(d) (void judgment); Transamerica Title Insurance Compnay v. Hendrix (1995) 34 Cal. App. 4th 740 (reversing trial court's denial of motion to set aside under similar cirumstancesDefendant has shown he has a meritorious case, a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action, and acted diligently to set aside the default judgment once discovered. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT DAVID ROBISON VS. CITGO PETROLEUM CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Joint Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendants To Second Set Of Joint Request For Production Of Documents; Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 16, 2012, Line PLAINTIFF SCOTT ROBISON'S Notice Of Motion And Joint Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendants To Second Set Of Joint Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WILSON POWELL et al VS. DOUGLAS R. LIVINGSTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 16, 2012, Line 4. CROSS COMPLAINANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Denied without prejudice to being re-filed on proper notice and in the proper form if the trial date is continued. The motion, along with the four other motions for summary adjudication, violates California Rule of Court 3.1113(d) limiting the number of pages of a memorandum filed in support of summary adjudication motion absent court order. The five separately filed motions are, in actuality, a single motion with separate issues that are, to a large extent, based on the same or similar set of facts. Even if the court did not have significantly reduced resources due to recent funding cutbacks, the court does not have the ability to read so many pages (many of which are duplicative or near-duplicative) for a single motion in a single case. Moreover, a cursory review of the motions disclose that the issues are neither so complex nor novel that they could not be adequately addressed within the applicable page limits or at least many fewer pages than comprise the five moving memoranda. Therefore, the court exercises its discretion not to consider the moving memoranda for any of the motions and thus denies the motions. (CRC 3.1113(g) (A memorandum exceeding the page limits is treated like a late-filed paper); Hobson v. Raychem Corporation (1999) 73 Cal. App. 4th 614, 623 (a court has the discretion to refuse to consider late-filed papers); CRC 3.113(a) (a moving memorandum is required for a summary adjudication motion). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 16, 2012, Line 5. DEFENDANT VERNON LOUCKS AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied in its entirety. There are triable issues whether and when defendants converted plaintiff's stock. Plaintiff has standing because it is complaining about harm that is particular to it, not merely corporate harm. There is a triable issue whether Vernon Loucks was a majority shareholder and whether Charles Loucks was the managing member. There is a triable issue whether defendants breached their tort and contractual duties to plaintiff. Lillienthal does not apply to the breach of fiduciary cause of action. That claim arises from a single train of events and involves a single duty. Even if Lillienthal applies to the contract claim, there are triable issues whether Comdisco owned the same amount of units and whether defendants violated section 4.5.2 by disposing of "substantially all of the Company" without plaintiff's approval. The triable issues of material fact exist even if the declaration of James Ciardelli is not considered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 16, 2012, Line 5. DEFENDANT VERNON LOUCKS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Denied. The motion is procedurally improper. The third cause of action seeks damages and defendants do not address that issue. Nor do defendants state a proper claim for declaratory relief. The declaration that defendants seek is not necessary or proper because the release can and should be raised as a defense. The motion violates CCP 437c(f)(1) because defendants are really attempting to dispose of less than the entirety of one or more of plaintiff's claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM A OSWALD VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus (Ccp 1094.5)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 16, 2012, Line 8. PETITIONER WILLIAM OSWALD Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus (Ccp 1094.5)Granted. The City's agreement that petitioner is 74% disabled as a result of the 2006 injury to his back requires that petitioner's disability retirement percentage also be 74% per section A8.598-3 of the San Francisco Charter notwithstanding that the 74% includes injuries to parts of petitioner's body other than his back. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
